channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Erich Zirnhelt
channel.itunes:summary:
How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
Community Accountability: Cariboo Signals scores 18/20 (Exemplary) on the Community Content Compact — Track A (Community-Insider production). Transparency: 4/4 · Source Integrity: 3/3 · Displacement: 2/2 · Consent & Attribution: 3/4 · Benefit Flow: 4/5 · Accountability: 2/2. Produced by Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC (Secwépemc territory). Corrections and PSA opt-outs: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Community Content Compact: 18/20 Exemplary (Track A). Self-funded, non-commercial. Producer: Erich Zirnhelt, Horsefly, BC. Corrections: feedback@cariboosignals.ca.

trace:assessment.trace:community: Rural Cariboo region, BC — Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh territories

item.title: Introducing The Show
item.description:
New here? Start with this one. Riley and Casey introduce themselves, explain how the show is actually put together, and walk you through what to expect each day of the week.

Meet the hosts: Riley (tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (community-first skeptic of tech promises) — both AI-generated, and upfront about it. Claude (Anthropic) writes the scripts, OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) voices them day to day (Riley = Nova, Casey = Echo), with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) run in parallel for quality comparisons, and the finished audio is mixed with music in Python (pydub). Cariboo Signals self-assesses at 18/20 ("Exemplary") on the Community Content Compact, a transparency framework for AI-made content.

What to expect, day by day:

  • Monday — Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
  • Tuesday — Working Lands & Industry: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
  • Wednesday — Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech: Consumer devices, maker projects, homelab, practical tools — technology you can hold, build, or deploy at home or on the job
  • Thursday — Indigenous Lands & Innovation: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
  • Friday — Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
  • Saturday — Cariboo Local Affairs: Williams Lake council, CRD, SD27, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — civic decisions, community voices, and local development that shape everyday life in the Cariboo
  • Sunday — Science, Wonder & the Natural World: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo

New episodes land every day — explore the back catalog, and subscribe to get tomorrow's as soon as it's ready.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) (with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) for comparison)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
item.itunes:summary:
New here? Start with this one. Riley and Casey introduce themselves, explain how the show is actually put together, and walk you through what to expect each day of the week.

Meet the hosts: Riley (tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (community-first skeptic of tech promises) — both AI-generated, and upfront about it. Claude (Anthropic) writes the scripts, OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) voices them day to day (Riley = Nova, Casey = Echo), with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) run in parallel for quality comparisons, and the finished audio is mixed with music in Python (pydub). Cariboo Signals self-assesses at 18/20 ("Exemplary") on the Community Content Compact, a transparency framework for AI-made content.

What to expect, day by day:

  • Monday — Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
  • Tuesday — Working Lands & Industry: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
  • Wednesday — Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech: Consumer devices, maker projects, homelab, practical tools — technology you can hold, build, or deploy at home or on the job
  • Thursday — Indigenous Lands & Innovation: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
  • Friday — Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
  • Saturday — Cariboo Local Affairs: Williams Lake council, CRD, SD27, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — civic decisions, community voices, and local development that shape everyday life in the Cariboo
  • Sunday — Science, Wonder & the Natural World: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo

New episodes land every day — explore the back catalog, and subscribe to get tomorrow's as soon as it's ready.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo) (with Azure Neural TTS (Ava · Andrew) for comparison)
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Science, Wonder And The Natural World
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Science, Wonder & the Natural World.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Business Insider] The AI industry's profit problem is getting bigger, says Goldman Sachs' equity research chief and [Business Insider] I flew on a startup's one-of-a-kind electric plane that could reshape air travel by 2027, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can AI tools reshape ecological science in the Cariboo in ways that serve community interests, or do they primarily amplify existing extractive research structures? Topics include: Infrastructure costs of AI and ecological impact of data centres, Indigenous data sovereignty and structural exclusion from peer-reviewed research systems, Edge-deployed vs. cloud-based AI models for environmental monitoring, Community-owned data licensing and fee-for-access ecological knowledge systems, Funding and sustainability models for long-term community AI governance infrastructure.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] The AI industry's profit problem is getti...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I flew on a startup's one-of-a-kind elect...
  • Maria Popova (The Marginalian): [The Marginalian] Ursula K. Le Guin on the Meaning of Life
  • Laisa Conde (Quesnel Cariboo Observer): [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George RCMP confirm 3 rece...
  • The Narwhal: [The Narwhal] In New Brunswick, residents battle the governm...

Additional sources provided:

  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/alyse-stanley (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Netflix just added a batch of feel-good movies ...
  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] The forgotten organ that could predict how lo...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/nikita-achanta (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I ditched my JBL Go 5 for the $49 8BitDo Retro ...
  • The Conversation (ScienceAlert): [ScienceAlert] AI Could Soon Use More Water Than Humanity Dr...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] ‘Elections campaigned on who can be mor...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] ‘Pervasive fear’ grips Gaza as Israeli ...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/lance-ulanoff (TechRadar): [TechRadar] I just watched a robot kick a child and I'm over...
  • Words and photographs by Pelumi Salako in Yawota (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] Gunshots at 9am. Then they...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] US says Iranian radar sites hit in Goru...
  • jenzielinski (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Locked out Kelowna daycare files sui...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Some job seekers are running out of money...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Kyiv residents seek shelter during rene...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Tired of AI making stuff up? This assist...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/amanda-caswell (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I tested local AI vs. ChatGPT side-by-side — he...

Closing Music: “So Called Democracy” by Stephane TV — rock, indie, indierock. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Cariboo Local Affairs
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Local Affairs.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [100 Mile Free Press] Stunting driver pulled over, ticketed near Lac La Hache and [Williams Lake Tribune] Openly transgender woman announces intent to run for Williams Lake City Council, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Can Williams Lake convert multiple simultaneous civic initiatives (jail forum, chamber crime resolution, municipal election) into meaningful outcomes, or is it activity without momentum? Topics include: Williams Lake jail closure forum and provincial political authority, Chamber of Commerce crime resolution escalation strategy, 2026 municipal election and candidate specificity on public safety, Infrastructure maintenance and active transportation initiatives, Community expertise, resilience, and the limits of knowledge extraction without compensation.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel & District Hospice Palliative Care Association

Sources discussed:

  • Patrick Davies (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] Stunting driver pulled over, ticketed ...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Openly transgender woman announces i...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake council backs forum on...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] BC Chamber of Commerce endorses crim...
  • Barbara Roden (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Lytton plays host to emerging leader...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Contractor set to begin paving between Rose...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Get active, reduce Greenhouse gas emissions...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Prince George-North Cariboo MLA applauds hi...
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Quesnel Search and Rescue respond to distre...

Additional sources provided:

  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] BC Hydro relocates two Williams Lake...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Three AI giants are on track to upend the...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Gian Zanette
  • Patrick Davies (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] PAPER QUIPS: Do you remember when…?
  • My Cariboo Now: [My Cariboo Now] Dad's Big Day Giveaway - Announcement : My ...

Closing Music: “Street Signs and White Lies” by madelyn munsell — pop, indie. Free music via Jamendo, licensed under Creative Commons.

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API
Weather Data: Open-Meteo
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [TechRadar] 'Who wouldn't want a large-scale industrial polluter sitting in their backyard?': California residents ban data centers as public backlash grows and [Android Authority] Walmart’s Onn accidentally leaks its cheaper alternative to Google Nest Cam with Floodlight, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How should communities balance AI data center development promises against environmental and landscape protection, and who should have decision-making authority? Topics include: Grid capacity and water demand impacts of data center expansion, Overstated permanent employment claims in tech industry siting proposals, Tŝilhqot'in Aboriginal title as legal protection mechanism, Regulatory capacity gaps in BC industrial approvals, Landscape and cultural value as unformalized stakes in approvals processes.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Chilcotin Conservation Society

Sources discussed:

  • https://www.techradar.com/author/david-nield (TechRadar): [TechRadar] 'Who wouldn't want a large-scale industrial poll...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Walmart’s Onn accidentally leaks its che...
  • Gail Sherman (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Scientists use yeast from body of Ötzi the Ice...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Satya Nadella defends Microsoft AI data c...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceDaily: [ScienceDaily] Typhoon Jangmi’s giant eye lights up the nigh...
  • Yashraj Sharma (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] How India’s CBSE exam scandal set off s...
  • Sheri Regnier (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Rossland hill closure linked to sear...
  • https://refractor.io/team/jay-kakade/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Intelligent 'krakens' really did rule the seas 1...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Richmond RCMP officer charged with t...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Accident on Kuwait motorway as Iranian ...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/ross-kelly (TechRadar): [TechRadar] An ode to Ask Jeeves - the iconic search engine ...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Mexico and Canada push to extend USMCA ...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Tunisian court sentences Ennahdha leade...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Indonesia arrests officials in crackdow...
  • Steve Dent (Engadget): [Engadget] Amazon kills plans for Stargate series reboot
  • Patrick Davies (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] South Cariboo artist’s 40 year artist ...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Kevin O'Leary says he's 'not walking away...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Grief, sobriety, music: Williams Lake native Petty Boy Osley’s journey and [Global News] Red Earth, Shoal Lake Cree Nation evacuees home as wildfire situation improves, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities build genuine leadership and governance authority over technology systems rather than merely participating in systems designed by others? Topics include: Leadership vs. participation in Indigenous tech governance, Brain drain and retention mechanisms in capacity-building programs, Governance authority as prerequisite for sustainable Indigenous tech development, Data sovereignty and benefit-sharing frameworks for territorial knowledge, Procurement policy as tool for structural advantage and self-sustainability.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • Yashvika Grover (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Grief, sobriety, music: Williams Lak...
  • Global News: [Global News] Red Earth, Shoal Lake Cree Nation evacuees hom...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/craig-hale (TechRadar): [TechRadar] EU to back European alternatives to US dominated...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Out-of-control 1,300-hectare wildfir...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake TRU graduates celebrat...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Psst - Jeff’s hosting a circle on June 17th
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Six new projects to explore impacts of plastic pollution on ...
  • Jake Howarth (Newsletter: n***@animikii.com): Yukon founder joins national Indigenous AI program as Firewe...

Additional sources provided:

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Improving safety for Indigenous communities
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Kuwait releases CCTV footage of deadly ...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] Ukraine drone attack kills 7 in Russian...
  • https://www.theguardian.com/profile/sarah-johnson (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] The race for oil: will Jam...
  • Brittany Webster (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Sentence upheld for man guilty of st...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Wilbert “Robbie” Merel Robichaud
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Can a US president promote stock they’v...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] UK far-right accused of exploiting kill...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada pumps money into forestry as ...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/grace-dean (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I perform a 'night flush' on my home to beat th...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Three British navy personnel killed in ...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] FBI agents fatally shoot alleged hostag...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API (Nova · Echo)
Web Search: Brave Search API

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Grief, sobriety, music: Williams Lake native Petty Boy Osley’s journey and [Global News] Red Earth, Shoal Lake Cree Nation evacuees home as wildfire situation improves, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can Indigenous communities build genuine leadership and governance authority over technology systems rather than merely participating in systems designed by others? Topics include: Leadership vs. participation in Indigenous tech governance, Brain drain and retention mechanisms in capacity-building programs, Governance authority as prerequisite for sustainable Indigenous tech development, Data sovereignty and benefit-sharing frameworks for territorial knowledge, Procurement policy as tool for structural advantage and self-sustainability.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • Yashvika Grover (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Grief, sobriety, music: Williams Lak...
  • Global News: [Global News] Red Earth, Shoal Lake Cree Nation evacuees hom...
  • https://www.techradar.com/author/craig-hale (TechRadar): [TechRadar] EU to back European alternatives to US dominated...
  • Laisa Conde (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Out-of-control 1,300-hectare wildfir...
  • rebeccadyok (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake TRU graduates celebrat...
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Psst - Jeff’s hosting a circle on June 17th
  • Newsletter: n***@animikii.com: Six new projects to explore impacts of plastic pollution on ...
  • Jake Howarth (Newsletter: n***@animikii.com): Yukon founder joins national Indigenous AI program as Firewe...

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Improving safety for Indigenous communities

  • https://www.theguardian.com/profile/sarah-johnson (The Guardian Global Development): [The Guardian Global Development] The race for oil: will Jam...
  • Brittany Webster (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Sentence upheld for man guilty of st...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Wilbert “Robbie” Merel Robichaud
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Can a US president promote stock they’v...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] UK far-right accused of exploiting kill...
  • none (Williams Lake Tribune): [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada pumps money into forestry as ...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/grace-dean (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I perform a 'night flush' on my home to beat th...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Three British navy personnel killed in ...
  • Al Jazeera Staff (Al Jazeera English): [Al Jazeera English] FBI agents fatally shoot alleged hostag...

item.title: Gear, Gadgets And Practical Tech
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Gear, Gadgets & Practical Tech.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] Testing LFP Battery Failure Modes With Overcharging and [The Verge] You can print, slice, and engrave using this desktop crafting machine, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can communities regain control over technology systems designed to be opaque and dependent, and what role should individual versus collective ownership play in that reclamation? Topics include: Cyberdeck DIY movement as response to surveillance-by-design in consumer devices, Right-to-repair versus device sovereignty as different upstream/downstream problems, 3D-printed construction as labor-cost solution for rural housing crises, Digital sovereignty for First Nations and Indigenous data management, Institutional procurement policies and technology governance frameworks.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Literacy Quesnel Society

Sources discussed:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Testing LFP Battery Failure Modes With Overchargi...
  • Andrew Liszewski (The Verge): [The Verge] You can print, slice, and engrave using this des...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] A Clock Inspired by Failed Cognitive Tests
  • CNET: [CNET] E-Bike Fires Are Down, but Riders Still Make One Dang...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Just as fitness trackers get interesting...
  • CNET: [CNET] I Hope Intel's Arc G3 Chips for Windows Gaming Handhe...

Additional sources provided:

  • Mario Aguilar (STAT News): [STAT News] STAT+: Blood pressure tech floods the market aft...
  • Andrew Liszewski (The Verge): [The Verge] This sound card could give gamers a competitive ...
  • CNET: [CNET] Best Windows Antivirus 2026: Keep Your Devices Safe W...

  • https://newatlas.com/author/monica-j-white/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Tiny EDC flashlight puts pro-grade LEDs on your ...
  • CNET: [CNET] There's a Heck of a Camera Inside This Colorfully Des...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/c-c-weiss/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Captivating stilted geometric tent takes on Amer...
  • Al Jazeera English: [Al Jazeera English] Ebola treatment centre rebuilt after be...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/jane-mcguire (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I’ve worn the new Oura Ring 5, and it really is...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/adam-williams/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] Record-breaking apartment building 3D printed in...
  • Amanda Silberling (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Cyberdecks are having a moment, rejecting big t...

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Boing Boing] Harvard students cheer graduation speaker encouraging them to mercilessly destroy AI and [The Tyee] BC’s Family Doctor Crisis Gets Even Worse, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What are the fundamental operational and structural gaps preventing Cariboo agricultural producers from accessing modern tools, markets, and infrastructure—and which gaps are actually solvable? Topics include: Agricultural-grade weather data infrastructure and mesonet models, Processing capacity and cold-chain infrastructure constraints for direct-to-consumer/regional beef markets, Blockchain-based traceability systems and provenance verification for regional producers, First Nations agricultural enterprises as alternative capital and land-tenure models, Extension service capacity and co-operative proposal-development as institutional gaps.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance

Sources discussed:

  • Rob Beschizza (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Harvard students cheer graduation speaker enco...
  • Andrew MacLeod (The Tyee): [The Tyee] BC’s Family Doctor Crisis Gets Even Worse
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] Amid a slew of chemical accidents, the age...
  • https://newatlas.com/author/david-szondy/ (New Atlas): [New Atlas] SR-71 successor? Quarterhorse Mk 2.1 prototype c...
  • Anna Heim (TechCrunch): [TechCrunch] Payroll startup Remote says it grew revenue 50%...

Additional sources provided:

  • https://www.facebook.com/CNBC (CNBC): [CNBC] Berkshire Hathaway invests extra $10 billion in Alpha...
  • Jordy Cunningham (100 Mile Free Press): [100 Mile Free Press] Fore: Golfer struck by cart in Kelowna
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/jason-england (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] I just held the most beautiful laptop, but I’m ...
  • Taipei Times: [Taipei Times] Hon Hai partners with France’s Bull for AI ex...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/david-crookes (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Want a new PC but hate current prices? Here's 5...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] I’m trying to cut down my subscriptions,...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Premier’s, parliamentary secretary’s statement...
  • https://www.tomsguide.com/author/zoran-danilovic (Toms Guide): [Toms Guide] Tech companies are scraping your personal data ...
  • CNET: [CNET] Today's NYT Strands Hints, Answers and Help for June ...
  • Maria Popova (The Marginalian): [The Marginalian] Music, the Neural Harmonics of Emotion, an...
  • Nathan Yau (FlowingData): [FlowingData] U.S. names with the oldest population
  • Rob Beschizza (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Loony Toon sentenced to 20 years in prison for...
  • Jason Weisberger (Boing Boing): [Boing Boing] Man who stole $11 million from prison has a ne...
